skill.name: security-bluebook-builder
skill.description: Build security Blue Books for sensitive apps
skill.summary: Create comprehensive security documentation (Blue Books) for sensitive applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs and weak cryptographic algorithms, but evaluation reveals these are false positives. The URLs are legitimate documentation links to the skill's GitHub repository. The cryptographic flag was triggered by the word 'build' in 'build security blue books', which refers to documentation creation, not cryptography. This is a simple documentation skill with no security concerns.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged URLs at lines 4 and 22 as hardcoded network references. These are legitimate documentation links to the skill's GitHub repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These URLs are benign documentation links in the skill metadata and markdown. No network requests are made by this skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'build' as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a false positive - the skill is about building security documentation ('Blue Books'), not cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word 'build' appears in the context of creating documentation, not cryptographic operations. This is a documentation skill with no cryptographic functionality.

content.value_statement: Create comprehensive security documentation for sensitive applications. This skill provides patterns and guidance for building security Blue Books that document security controls, threat models, and compliance requirements.
actual_capabilities: Generate security documentation templates for applications
Provide guidance on security control documentation
Document security requirements and testing procedures
limitations: Does not execute security scans or tests
Does not generate actual code or implementation
Does not provide real-time security monitoring
Requires human review for accuracy and completeness

use_cases.title: New Application Security Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate initial security Blue Book structure for a new sensitive application, including security controls, threat model sections, and compliance mappings.
use_cases.target_user: Security engineers and architects documenting new applications

use_cases.title: Compliance Documentation Update
use_cases.description: Refresh existing security documentation to meet new compliance requirements or update threat model sections.

use_cases.title: Security Documentation Review
use_cases.description: Review and improve existing security Blue Books for completeness and accuracy.

prompt_templates.title: Create New Blue Book
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a security Blue Book for my new application [application name]. It handles [sensitive data types] and is deployed on [infrastructure]. Generate a comprehensive documentation structure including threat model, security controls, and compliance sections.

prompt_templates.title: Add Threat Model Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a threat model section to the existing security Blue Book for [application]. Include sections for assets, threat actors, attack vectors, and mitigation strategies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expanding existing documentation with threat modeling

prompt_templates.title: Map Compliance Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a compliance mapping section for [regulation/standard] in our security Blue Book. Map our existing security controls to the required compliance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Review Security Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the security controls section of our Blue Book for [application]. Identify gaps, suggest improvements, and ensure coverage of OWASP Top 10 and common attack vectors.

output_examples.input: Create a security Blue Book for a healthcare application that handles patient data
output_examples.output:
Security Blue Book Outline:

1. Executive Summary
2. Application Overview
3. Data Classification (PHI, PII)
4. Threat Model
   - Assets: Patient records, authentication
   - Threat Actors: External attackers, insider threats
   - Attack Vectors: SQL injection, XSS, privilege escalation
5. Security Controls
   - Access Control
   - Encryption (AES-256 for data at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit)
   - Audit Logging
6. Compliance Mapping (HIPAA)
7. Incident Response Procedures

output_examples.input: Add threat model to our financial transaction processing system
output_examples.output:
Threat Model Section:

## Assets
- Transaction data
- User credentials
- API keys

## Threat Actors
- External attackers
- Malicious insiders
- Automated bots

## Attack Vectors
- API abuse
- Credential stuffing
- Data exfiltration

## Mitigation Strategies
- Rate limiting
- MFA enforcement
- DLP controls

best_practices: Start with a clear data classification section to define what needs protection
Include both technical controls and procedural security measures
Regularly update threat models as the application evolves
anti_patterns: Copy-pasting generic security templates without customization
Ignoring non-functional security requirements like incident response
Failing to document security decisions and their rationale

faq.question: What is a security Blue Book?
faq.answer: A security Blue Book is comprehensive documentation that outlines an application's security controls, threat models, compliance requirements, and security architecture decisions.

faq.question: Does this skill perform security testing?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates documentation templates and guidance. It does not execute security scans or tests.

faq.question: Which compliance standards does this support?
faq.answer: The skill can help document compliance with various standards including SOC 2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, and ISO 27001.

faq.question: Can I use this for cloud infrastructure documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides guidance for documenting security controls for cloud-based applications and infrastructure.

faq.question: How detailed is the generated documentation?
faq.answer: The skill generates comprehensive outlines and templates. You should expand each section with specific details about your application.

faq.question: Does this work for microservices architectures?
faq.answer: Yes, the documentation templates can be adapted for microservices, including service-to-service security and API security sections.
